reference_audit.notes: Tests use only in-scope imported RFC 9000 IDs. No source-side requirement refs were present in the scoped code, which is consistent with the repository convention for this slice.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no packet-assembly surface that can guarantee one or more frames in every QUIC packet.
requirements.notes: Packet composition is still outside the available helper slice.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no send-path batching scheduler that can wait briefly to coalesce frames before emitting an underfilled packet.
requirements.notes: Needs the outgoing packet scheduler, not just ACK-state helpers.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no heuristic-based packet pacing surface that can decide how long to wait before sending.
requirements.notes: This depends on application-aware send timing.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no packet composer that can pack multiple STREAM frames into one QUIC packet.
requirements.notes: The current work only covers ACK generation, not general packet packing.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no packet packing policy that can prefer fewer active streams while preserving transmission efficiency.
requirements.notes: Requires the same packet-assembly surface as the other S13 opening clauses.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no decryption-complete / frame-processing completion hook that can delay ACKing until all packet contents are processed.
requirements.notes: ACK tracking starts after the helper is told a packet has been processed.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no STREAM enqueue / delivery lifecycle surface to distinguish application enqueue from app-consumption.
requirements.notes: The helper does not model STREAM frame delivery to the application.
The helper records processed packets and the generated ACK frame round-trips through the frame codec, including the largest acknowledged packet and ack delay.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no incoming-ACK validation surface that can raise PROTOCOL_VIOLATION for acknowledgments of unsent packets.
requirements.notes: This belongs in inbound ACK processing, which is not present in the chunk.
The helper records processed packets and the generated ACK frame round-trips through the frame codec, including the largest acknowledged packet and ack delay.

The helper delays ACK emission for non-ack-eliciting traffic, attempts to piggyback ACKs with other outgoing frames, and only decides to ACK immediately after the second ack-eliciting packet or the delay timer elapses.

The helper delays ACK emission for non-ack-eliciting traffic, attempts to piggyback ACKs with other outgoing frames, and only decides to ACK immediately after the second ack-eliciting packet or the delay timer elapses.

The helper delays ACK emission for non-ack-eliciting traffic, attempts to piggyback ACKs with other outgoing frames, and only decides to ACK immediately after the second ack-eliciting packet or the delay timer elapses.
Ack-eliciting application-data packets are held to max_ack_delay, which also applies to 0-RTT and 1-RTT application-data traffic in the helper-level model.
The helper records processed packets and the generated ACK frame round-trips through the frame codec, including the largest acknowledged packet and ack delay.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no recovery timer surface that uses the receiver max_ack_delay in PTO or retransmission timeout calculations.
requirements.notes: This is a QUIC recovery concern rather than ACK generation state.
Ack-eliciting application-data packets are held to max_ack_delay, which also applies to 0-RTT and 1-RTT application-data traffic in the helper-level model.
Initial and Handshake packets are marked for immediate ACK generation as soon as they are recorded.
The helper enforces the one-ACK-only-packet-per-ack-eliciting-packet guard.
The helper-level guard now has requirement-home proof coverage and canonical xrefs.
The helper records processed packets and the generated ACK frame round-trips through the frame codec, including the largest acknowledged packet and ack delay.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no peer-side ACK reception model to show that ACK-only traffic will not itself be acknowledged.
requirements.notes: This is a consequence of peer behavior, not local ACK generation.

The helper delays ACK emission for non-ack-eliciting traffic, attempts to piggyback ACKs with other outgoing frames, and only decides to ACK immediately after the second ack-eliciting packet or the delay timer elapses.

The helper delays ACK emission for non-ack-eliciting traffic, attempts to piggyback ACKs with other outgoing frames, and only decides to ACK immediately after the second ack-eliciting packet or the delay timer elapses.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no feedback-loop model for adding ack-eliciting frames to otherwise non-ack-eliciting packets.
requirements.notes: Needs the general packet composer / send-path decision layer.
Out-of-order/gap-detecting packets and CE-marked packets trigger immediate ACK generation, and CE packets preserve ECN counts.
Out-of-order/gap-detecting packets and CE-marked packets trigger immediate ACK generation, and CE packets preserve ECN counts.

The ACK frame helper and codec preserve multiple ACK ranges, the largest received packet number, and the ACK Delay field without adding uncontrolled delay.

The helper trims the oldest retained ACK ranges when the configured limit is reached, keeping the newest ranges and the largest acknowledged packet number.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no peer-ACK lifecycle surface to retire ACK ranges after acknowledgments for an ACK frame are received.
requirements.notes: The helper can trim locally retained ranges, but it does not model received ACKs for ACK frames.

requirements.remaining_gap: Informational prose about expected ACK receipt rates; no executable behavior is attached to the clause.
requirements.notes: Deferred intentionally because it is descriptive rather than a distinct code path.

The ACK frame helper and codec preserve multiple ACK ranges, the largest received packet number, and the ACK Delay field without adding uncontrolled delay.

requirements.remaining_gap: Section marker / descriptive clause for Section 13.2.4, not a runtime rule.
requirements.notes: Deferred intentionally because no implementation hook is expected for the section heading.

requirements.remaining_gap: Informational note about ACK frame loss and reordering; no helper-level behavior can guarantee or violate it directly.
requirements.notes: Deferred intentionally because the clause describes a limit of the approach rather than a code path.
The helper records processed packets and the generated ACK frame round-trips through the frame codec, including the largest acknowledged packet and ack delay.
The ACK frame helper and codec preserve multiple ACK ranges, the largest received packet number, and the ACK Delay field without adding uncontrolled delay.
The helper reports ACK Delay directly from measured receive-to-send time, so no uncontrolled delay is introduced.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no decryption-key buffering state to add uncontrolled key-wait latency into ACK Delay.
requirements.notes: The helper only models locally measured receive-to-send delay.
The helper reports measured delay greater than max_ack_delay directly in ACK Delay and emits ACK-ECN frames when CE was observed.
ACK generation is tracked independently per packet number space, so the helper builds separate ACK state for Initial, Handshake, and ApplicationData traffic.
ACK generation is tracked independently per packet number space, so the helper builds separate ACK state for Initial, Handshake, and ApplicationData traffic.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no carrier-selection surface that can force client 0-RTT acknowledgments onto 1-RTT packets.
requirements.notes: The helper tracks packet number spaces but does not choose packet encryption levels.

requirements.remaining_gap: Informational consequence of delayed or lost server handshake messages; no separate implementation surface exists for the note itself.
requirements.notes: Deferred intentionally because the clause is explanatory, not a standalone rule.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no periodic non-PADDING probe / send-ack-eliciting-frames scheduler.
requirements.notes: The current slice does not own the periodic path-probe policy.

notes: No reconciliation artifact existed for this chunk; the implementation summary was treated as the source of truth.
The requirement-home proof for REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P2P1-0007 now closes the remaining gap and removes it from the blocked set.
The helper-level implementation covers ACK state, delayed ACK thresholds, immediate ACK triggers, ACK range trimming, ACK-ECN emission, per-space tracking, and the one-ACK-only-packet guard.
No stale or wrong requirement refs were found in the scoped source or tests.
